name: Naive MCP schema overhead
summary: Naive MCP clients inject full tools/list JSON Schema into every agent turn. GitHub surfaces differ: Copilot MCP (~43 tools) drives ~28k-44k tokens on simple tasks; the larger github-mcp-server (~93 tools) reports ~55k tokens idle before work. Stacking servers (e.g. three integrations) can consume ~143k tokens (~72% of a 200k window) before the first user question.

key_metrics.mechanism: Full tool schema injection each turn

methodology_notes: Mechanism analysis from Scalekit per-task measures, OnlyCLI idle registration counts, Firecrawl multi-server synthesis, Anthropic advanced-tool-use examples. Not all figures from a single controlled n; treat server variant as a first-class variable.
implications_for_gax: GAX must never preload full registries into context. Manifest index + gax doc/search targets ~80-250 tokens per discovery call. MCP servers should be wrapped as GAX adapters so schemas stay in gaxd, not the LLM transcript.
corrections_to_repo_docs: research/01: split 43-tool vs 93-tool figures; avoid single 44k-55k range without labels.
